title: Register a vehicle for a temporary stay in Barbados
description: Apply for an International Circulation Permit if you are bringing a vehicle into Barbados for a short stay.
summary: If you are visiting Barbados with your own vehicle for a short time, you must register it for the length of your stay. The Licensing Authority gives you an International Circulation Permit (ICP), which is your temporary registration.

You need an ICP if you are coming on holiday, on business or for a short work assignment with a vehicle that is registered overseas. Without it, your vehicle is not legally allowed on Barbadian roads.
Bring the overseas registration document, your overseas insurance certificate and any shipping documents. If you are travelling on a Carnet de Passage, bring that too.
The permit lasts for the length of your planned stay. If you decide to stay longer, you must apply to extend the ICP before it expires.

items: your full name, address, phone number and email
the length of your planned stay
the registration number, make, model and year of the vehicle
the engine number and chassis number

items.name: Overseas registration document for the vehicle
Carnet de Passage or shipping documents

fees: Fees depend on the length of stay and the vehicle class. Confirm at the Licensing Authority.
how_long: Permits are usually issued within 5 working days.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Registration of Vehicle for Temporary Stay form and your documents to the Barbados Licensing Authority.

contact.address:
The Pine, East–West Boulevard
St. Michael
BB11000

assumptions: ‘Permits are usually issued within 5 working days’ is an estimate.
Fee structure (‘depends on the length of stay and the vehicle class’) is general practice. The form itself does not list a fee schedule.
Document list (registration, insurance, Carnet de Passage, shipping documents) is based on common ICP practice, not the form.
